mnemonics.title: Graph Components
mnemonics.content: V ER: V for Vertices (the V shape), E for Edges, R for Relation - graphs define relations between vertices using edges

mnemonics.title: Handshaking Lemma
mnemonics.content: HANDSHAKE: Have A Nice Degree Sum - Handshaking Always Needs Degrees = 2 × Edges

mnemonics.title: Complete Graph Edges
mnemonics.content: K n choose 2: Knights (K) in chess choose (nC2) positions - remember n(n-1)/2 edges in complete graph

acronyms.usage: Used to denote the set of all vertices in graph G
Used to denote the set of all edges in graph G
Number of edges incident to vertex v
Graph where every pair of vertices is connected
Graph forming a simple cycle with n vertices
Graph forming a simple path with n vertices
Graph with same vertices but edges where G has no edges

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a round table where every person shakes hands with every other person - complete connections everywhere
Think of vertices as beads on a necklace, connected in a circular loop forming a closed chain
Think of a straight line of houses, each connected to its neighbors
Each edge contributes 1 to degree of each endpoint - imagine handshakes at a party, each handshake adds 2 to total handshakes count
Isolated = island (no connections), Pendant = leaf hanging from a tree branch (only one connection)

analogies.analogy: Like a social network: people are vertices, friendships are edges connecting them
Like one-way streets in a city - you can go from A to B but not necessarily back on the same road
Like road distances on a map - each road (edge) has a length (weight) associated with it
Everyone at a party knows everyone else - no strangers
Like a portion of a larger map - showing only some cities and roads connecting them
